steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes "veins that provide structural ___" and must also handle the transition to the second part: "[blank] in thickness from the center to the edges, these veins allow the leaves to maintain their large size and buoyancy…" The second part ("these veins allow…") is an independent clause, so whatever comes before it must correctly separate two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'support. Decreasing': Ends the first independent clause with a period. "Decreasing in thickness from the center to the edges" then opens the next sentence as a participial phrase correctly modifying "these veins." ✓
  • Choice B — 'support decreasing': Turns "decreasing" into a participial phrase directly attached to "veins that provide structural support decreasing in thickness" — garbled meaning — and leaves "these veins allow…" as a second independent clause with no separator. ✗
  • Choice C — 'support, while decreasing': Creates "veins that provide structural support, while decreasing in thickness … , these veins allow…" — the subject "these veins" is then separated from the main clause only by a comma, producing a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — 'support, decreasing': Same problem as C — the comma before "decreasing" does not prevent a comma splice when "these veins allow…" follows. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
